entry.title: SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 3 季度版本
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接 What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition, 原作者 EsmeeX。

你好，开发者！ 我们很高兴为您介绍 SAP Build 第 3 季度的新增功能系列。 在此版本中，我们将介绍针对以下内容的产品特定更新：

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start、SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

版本管理和项目生命周期管理：现在可以使用 SAP Cloud Transport Management 系统跨不同租户发布和传输项目。在隔离 SAP Build Apps 应用程序到其自有架构的开发、测试和生产部署时，您可以在子账户之间传输项目版本。要开始，请参阅此博客帖子。

包含 S/4HANA 中大型数据集的性能表列表：管理和显示应用程序中的大量数据可能具有挑战性并导致性能问题。查看此示例了解如何为具有 65k+ 记录的 S/4HANA Cloud 端点创建性能列表页面 - 请参阅博客帖子。

在一个项目中复制页面：此功能使用户能够在 SAP Build Apps 的项目中复制和复制页面，这是一个简单但功能强大的扩展组件，可进一步加快开发速度。

新的构建服务：我们最近推出了全新的改进体验来部署应用程序包。构建服务包括管理图像资产、iOS 使用字符串、移动服务启用、打包创建和配置管理。更新的 UI 在部署阶段为开发人员提供了更顺畅的体验，确保满足每个要求并且您的应用已准备好发布。试试吧！

  • 另请查看路多·诺恩斯 (Ludo Noens) 系列视频，了解如何借助 SAP Build Apps 使用 SAP 解决方案，创建 CRUD 应用
  • 详细了解 SAP Build Apps 路线图的最新动态。

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：SAP S/4HANA 公共云的 2408 版现在提供可扩展性向导。这使得并排扩展的创建更加简化，并允许您从cloud ERP 无缝导航到 SAP Build。第一个版本支持自定义流程创建，SAP Build 今年将提供更多选项。有关详细信息，请参阅博客。

Script Task Editor (脚本任务编辑器)：现在可以在新的脚本编辑器中编写 JavaScript 代码，从而允许在流程上下文中执行复杂的数据类型转换和附加操作。之前，这仅限于自动化 - 现在包括流程输入和自定义变量，有助于高级数据操作和转换。在博客帖子中查看更多信息。

计划触发器：现在，您可以在运行时根据用户定义的时间间隔和日期计划流程自动化。这允许您在创建自动化方案时启用以系统为中心和基于时间的流程自动化用例，例如工作日和非工作日，以避免资源不匹配。

表单中的附加结果和 UI5 任务支持：这使开发人员能够在流程中配置附加结果操作，以便在审批表单中缺少信息或信息错误时让审批人请求返工。您还可以通过引导式体验有效地开始填写多个表单。

通过手动说明上传创建高级事件项目：这允许用户上传业务事件规范，以在大厅中创建事件项目。新功能增加了对跨业务范围 (LOB) 和自定义事件的业务事件的支持，包括在运行时使用 SAP Event Mesh 支持的第三方事件。

流程可见性增强：包括切实可行的洞察、针对请求者的实例跟踪以及通过 CALM 提供的附加监控功能。现在，您可以将洞察转化为有意义的结果，并根据可见性洞察触发上报流，以直接响应和解决关键情况。这样可实现可审计和治理的上报解决方案，而不是在不同渠道断开通信。

了解有关 SAP Build Process Automation 的更多信息：

  • 有关更多更新和改进，请参阅 What's New Viewer（新增功能查看器）。
  • 有关更多即将推出的功能和重点区域，请参阅 SAP Road Map Explorer。

SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone（两个版本）

HTML5 业务解决方案作为内容提供者：通过 SAP Build Work Zone 中的最新更新，您现在可以以“通用数据模型”(CDM) 格式将基于 BTP 的应用程序公开为 Work Zone 内容，例如角色、空间和页面。因此，您可以轻松配置和启用从 Work Zone 实例访问 BTP 应用程序的功能，甚至可以跨子账户访问。 请参阅帮助门户和此博客帖子中的更多信息。

SAP Build Work Zone（标准版）

定义页面和空间的排列顺序：管理员现在可以配置在 SAP Build Work Zone（标准版）的菜单栏上对空间和页面进行排序。仅当在空间和页面的新体验查看模式(new experience view mode)下工作时，此功能才适用。请参阅帮助门户中的更多信息。

页面和空间的导入和导出：将增强内容管理器工具，使管理员能够在架构之间导出和导入自定义（本地创建的）空间和页面。

SAP Build Work Zone（高级版）

跨 SAP Build Work Zone 和 Process Automation 的集成引导式体验：现在，引导式流程可用作 SAP Build Process Automation 中的部件。除了之前可用的“无模板”向导外，现在还可以在 SAP Build Work Zone 中创建基于流程的引导式向导。请参阅博客帖子中的更多信息。

SAP Start

直接导航到集成云解决方案的产品主页：SAP Start 站点提供了一个新产品分区，可以轻松查看和导航到集成解决方案的产品主页。这使当前用户的可用云解决方案透明化，并快速轻松地导航以启动所需解决方案的产品主页。

SAP Task Center

ITCE 合作伙伴页面：SAP Task Center 现在允许与第三方任务提供者连接。利用此功能的第一个解决方案是由 ITCE 与 SAP 架构师和技术顾问合作开发的 TaskConnect。该应用程序很快将在 SAP Store 中提供，提供全面的配置指南、安装手册和 BPMN 图表。有关 TaskConnect 的详细信息，请参阅此处。

有关 SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center 的详细信息：

  • 查看 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版和高级版和 SAP Start/SAP Task Center 的路线图
  • 新增功能查看器：标准版/高级版/SAP Start/SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

新增的 AI 功能：在 SAP Build Code 中，您现在可以使用 Joule copilot（我们的生成式 AI 助手）通过提供自然语言提示编写代码。关于Joule的详细解释,请阅读此文。在过去几个月中，我们为此功能添加了许多新功能，包括以下亮点：

  • 支持面向数据模型和应用逻辑的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 服务集成：现在可以使用Joule为集成的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 服务生成代码。
  • SAP Build Code 中的 Joule 主要用户体验更新：借助 SAP Build Code 内的 Joule 的最新用户体验更新，我们提高了可用性，简化了工作流，并创建了更直观的界面，提高了生产力和满意度。
  • 生产力工具的生成式AI 支持：我们现在允许您直接从故事板使用Joule。
  • 使用Joule增强现有的数据模型和服务：现在可以使用提示修改现有的数据模型和服务实体。

为什么选择 SAP Build Code：查看产品经理 Rupa Maity 发布的博文，了解为什么迁移至 SAP Build Code。

了解最新的构建代码创新成果：您是否希望随时了解 SAP Build Code 的最新产品更新？在 SAP Community 上关注我们的标签，并订阅 SAP Build Code 简报。 

  • 请参阅 SAP Build Code 自 2024 年 9 月起的最新更新产品更新。
  • 了解有关 SAP Build Code 社区的更多信息。

--- 

 

请注意，此博客帖子提供了最新功能和增强的亮点。 

  • 有关所有更新的完整列表，请参阅 Help Portal SAP Build 中的新增内容。
  • 加入 SAP Builders group，获取产品资源和社区问题。
  • 不要错过 SAP Build-themed sessions 等。
  • 这意味着 SAP TechEd 就在眼前！在 2024 年 TechEd 大会上，深入了解 SAP Build。

您最想测试哪些功能？请在评论中告诉我们！

entry.title: SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build: what a great experience!
entry.content:
At the end of last week I had a very stimulating trip to Berlin, where I participated in the SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build. It was an interesting event and I'm happy to share my takeaways with you.

Thursday November 27, 8:30 AM: I join my colleagues who arrived from Walldorf and Helsinki for a breakfast at our hotel, and we quickly head up to SAP Berlin office. Once there, together with the Berlin colleagues, we set up the event rooms we had booked for the keynote and then the hands-on workshop.

At 10.30, attendees start to arrive: we have the pleasure to welcome business users and developers from Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe (BVG), Bundesdruckerei, Stiftung Das Rauhe Haus, Hochschule für Technik und Wirtschaft Berlin as well as partners and a few SAP employees. All together, we engage discussions over a buffet of snacks before the event kicks off at 11.00 with our keynote, joined by many attendees on LinkedIn live and moderated by Esmeé.

Keynote and solution deep dive

Diana Roesner starts the keynote by introducing the Clean Core concept and explaining how SAP Build can be used to easily create side-by-side extensions to an SAP application. On this very important topic, I highly recommend you a new white paper - “Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud” - and a crisp and easy-to consume learning journey - “Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud”.

We then dive into the different components of the SAP Build solution, with my close colleagues Kanishka presenting SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Code, and Yumnah describing SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Work Zone. Jesse gives a great demo of how SAP Build can help 🎅🏻 Santa Company 🎄 streamline order approvals by creating an extension directly from his SAP S/4HANA ERP using our Extensibility Wizard. Finally Hemant provides an insightful perspective on how SAP Build supports line-of-businesses, including finance, procurement, supply chain, customer experience, human resources, IT and cross-functional:

Following the presentations, our experts held a Q&A session with the audience, answering the questions raised on-site as well as in the online chat:

We received very positive feedback from the audience on this session, which you can watch as a replay.

You can also download the slides of this keynote.

Hands-on Workshop

After a short break, we moved to another room for the hands-on workshop. In 3 hours, our local participants had the opportunity to create a full-stack application to improve customer loyalty. The purpose of the application is to allow customers to redeem points from their purchases. To do this, the application needs to access customer, purchase and product data from SAP S/4HANA.

Specifically, workshop participants experienced using SAP Build Code to create a back-end CAP service with Joule AI copilot, highlighting SAP's generative AI power for development. The customer loyalty application is connected to SAP S/4HANA Cloud as an extension to the ERP, so the participants learned how to use a BTP destination. And they used SAP Build Apps to create a mobile app, showcasing our modern low-code/no-code development tool.

This is the part of the event I was involved in, and I have to say it was great and rewarding to see our participants amazed at how Joule helped them create data entities, sample data and JavaScript code from simple prompts.  They really experienced the power of Generative AI that we showcased at SAP TechEd a few weeks ago!

If you would like to do this hands-on exercise and experience Joule by yourself, it’s very easy!

Indeed, it is available here in our Basic Trial environment, where you will have immediate access to all the tools needed: SAP Build Code - Generative AI-powered development with Joule copilot.

Christmas Market at AGILITA’s

At 16:00 we jumped into a bus who drove us to the AGILITA office in Berlin. There, AGILITA Christmas Market brought everyone together to reflect and share ideas in a festive atmosphere.

Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience for AGILITA, presented SAP AppHaus Workshops: SAP and AGILITA are helping customers find the right path to digitalization with design and architecture thinking workshops. Customers discover how to integrate Al and innovation for their own use cases, and how to effectively improve their business processes.

We also had the opportunity to attend very interesting demos of applications developed by the AGILITA team with SAP Build, showing how tasks like recording time or logging work can happen in just 3 clicks:  

AGIL AI.TimeManager  - Optimize your time and activity recording with AGIL Time Manager, the flexible and user- friendly solution for efficient time management and seamless integration in real-time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.TimeManager in SAP Store.

 

AGIL Al.Sales  - Boost sales performance with the power of voice and Al synergy. Effortlessly dictate orders and meeting notes while on the go - advanced generative Al transcribes, analyzes, and seamlessly updates your systems in real time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.Sales in SAP Store.

 Discover more AGILITA’s applications and extensions in SAP Store.

Up to you now!

You too can take advantage of the content we have prepared for this event. And it's very simple!

  1. Watch the keynote replay 🎞  ️
  2. Download the presentation 👍🏻  
  3. Do the hands-on exercise 👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓

 

Personal note

To end this blog post on a more personal note, I would like to say that I had 2 very energizing days in Berlin. I only work 3 days a week, Monday-Wednesday, but when I was asked if I could attend this event in Berlin on a Thursday, I didn't hesitate for a second: as I work 100% from home in Lyon, it was a great opportunity to meet up with colleagues from Berlin and Walldorf. The day after the event, we took time to reflect on what we did in 2024 and what we would like to do in 2025. This trip to Berlin was also a nice opportunity to meet great people from our valued partner AGILITA.

And when I was on my way back to Lyon on Friday evening, what caught my eye in the Berlin airport terminal?

On a big screen, an ad from SAP promoting our magical trifecta of Process + Data + AI 😊

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What' s New in Q4 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q4 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance we have added new product pages.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add three new product pages that consolidate links to all available release communication channels, like schedules, pre- and post-release communication, blogs, and more.

The new pages are:

  1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  2. SAP Mobile Services
  3. SAP Translation Hub

Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, documentation on releases and available modules, the GitHub Kyma repositories, as well as a helpful learning journey and hands-on tutorials.

Check it out.

Fig 1. The new page for SAP BTP, Kyma runtime.

 

2. SAP Mobile Services

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 2. The new page for SAP Mobile Services.

 

3. SAP Translation Hub

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 3. The new page for SAP Translation Hub.

 

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 30 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

 

Please note that the product page for SAP Data Intelligence Cloud has been retired as of this update. For more information, you can refer to this blog post.

 

For centralized information on when SAP BTP products and services release new features refer to the Consolidated Release Schedule for SAP BTP.

Regarding Release Navigator updates, you can refer to this update plan:

*Disclaimer:  The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.

Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

 

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

 

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Extending SLP functionalities with SAP BTP and Artificial Intelligence
entry.content:
Problem: Currently have a lot of human interventions in the review and analysis of suppliers in the initial supplier registration process.

Solution: In the supplier registration process we can automate the review of documentation, specific fields and even extract data from documents such as PDFs and images using Document Information Extraction 

How:

A. Using SAP Ariba APIs to: approve/deny pending tasks and get task and supplier details, get/update the supplier registration questionnaire, complete internal forms and then approve/deny

B. Using  SAP Build Process Automation to manage APIs, process information with artificial intelligence, manage forms and tasks, and execute the process periodically and/or on demand

C. Using SAP AI Core , or any other foundational model supplier dedicated as an AI agent providing the details of the supplier registration questionnaire to validate:

1. The information provided in the registration questionnaire 

2. To give a high-level analysis about the supplier (sentiment rating)

Here is the architecture to be followed

For this specific scenario the following APIs are being used:

External Approval API for Sourcing and Supplier Management endpoints: pendingApprovables(Get), Task(Get),  Workspace(Get), action(Post)

Supplier Data API With Pagination endpoint: vendors(Post)
 
At Build Process Automation the following flow is being developed

If you are new in SAP Build Process Automation check this Blog - How-To: Use Script Tasks in SAP Build Process Automation  were the basics are defined.

APIs can be called from a custom Script, however as a best practice I highly recommend create a Destination and at Build Process Automation create an Action to be reused at the process flow 

 "Prepare Call Options" Automation will have a custom script:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

With these steps we can now obtain the details of the registration questionnaire in order to make inquiries about documents or specific information. For example, SAP Build Process Automation has the PDF SDK preconfigured which allows us to manipulate this type of documents to extract and manage data. However we can use the Document Processing and Information Extraction which allow us to process data from structured or semistructured information from documents using machine learning models(OCR, Pretrained Models and Templates)

On the other hand, the information obtained can be processed with AI agents, such as SAP AI Core, which can be trained with specific information from suppliers or we can consume information from third-party services specialized in supplier evaluation and management.
The prompt to extract the information could look like this:

As you can see in the example prompt, we can even predefine the response to easily obtain the desired data to insert it into the indicated forms or use it to make decisions on the approval/denial of a form.

Once we have the information processed, it is time to return to SAP Ariba SLP to approve/reject forms or request more information from the supplier in case the information provided is incorrect or incomplete.
 
More Information Requested - Example

Internal form completed and approved by the information generated by AI - Example

Internal form completed and approved by the information generated by AI - Example

Conclusion

Having said all this, we can use SAP Ariba APIs to extract information but also to update and create content. On the other hand, BTP has tools created that easily adapt to different business needs, all with the aim of automating processes inside and outside SAP solutions.

 

More Information About:

APIs

Blog - Consume Ariba APIs using Postman
SAP Ariba Developer Portal
SAP Ariba API Documentation

SAP Build Process Automation

Discovery Center

entry.title: Infusing AI into Journal Entry Process - Beyond Automation
entry.content:
Background:

During critical periods such as month-end, quarter-end, or year-end close, organizations often face a high volume of manual journal entries being posted into their S/4  systems. These entries can sometimes contain errors, especially when they are entered by various users who might not have a consistent understanding of the required formats, values, or related documentation.

Currently, these manual entries are posted directly into the system without prior checks, leading to potential discrepancies, accounting errors, or compliance violations. These errors could include incorrect amounts, incorrect GL codes, misaligned descriptions, missing or inaccurate supporting documentation, and others. To mitigate this risk, a more streamlined and controlled process is needed where the journal entries are parked first, reviewed for validation, and then posted after being confirmed as correct.

Key Issues:

  1. Manual Journal Entry Errors: These include issues such as:
    • Incorrect amounts posted for a particular entry type (e.g., a cost that is typically $5,000 being entered as $500).
    • Incorrect General Ledger (GL) codes or descriptions (e.g., the GL code does not match the standard or policy documentation).
    • Missing or incorrect attachment references (e.g., an invoice or supporting document is either not attached or is attached to the wrong entry).
    • Missing or incomplete information (e.g., missing approval, insufficient details for audit).
  2. Lack of Validation Prior to Posting: Since the journal entries are directly posted into the system, there is no opportunity to detect these errors before they are committed to the books, leading to potential audit issues, compliance risks, and manual corrections that are costly and time-consuming.

Solution Objective: To streamline this process, the goal is to implement a system where all manual journal entries are first "parked" (held temporarily) in the system, rather than posted immediately. During this parked state, the entries can undergo a series of automated checks or validations, leveraging historical data, company policy documents, and attachments to ensure accuracy and compliance. The system can then generate alerts or notifications to flag any anomalies, providing users with the opportunity to correct them before final posting.

Key Components: A Symphony of Technology

SAP BTP AI Services

Leverage pre-built AI models for document analysis, text extraction, and machine learning. These services enable intelligent data processing and automation.

S/4HANA

The core ERP system provides access to real-time financial data, enabling accurate and efficient journal entry creation and posting.

Data Pipelines

Securely transport data between BTP AI and S/4HANA, ensuring smooth and reliable integration of processed data for journal entries.

AI-Enabled Validation System:

An AI-enabled solution would help in detecting these anomalies by analyzing historical data, policy documents, and attachment details. The AI system would focus on the following validation areas:

  1. Amount Validation:
    • Historical Amount Comparison: AI can learn typical amounts for certain types of journal entries based on historical data. For example, if a specific type of entry (e.g., payroll) typically has an amount in the range of $4,500 to $5,500, the system can flag entries that are too high or too low.
    • Amount Consistency: Cross-checking the amounts with similar entries from previous periods or other business units to ensure consistency.
  2. GL Code Validation:
    • GL Code Matching: Ensuring that the correct GL code is used based on the entry type. If an entry for "supplies" is posted under a GL code used for "payroll", it can be flagged.
    • GL Code Description Match: The AI can validate that the description associated with the GL code aligns with the standard naming convention or description used in the policy documents.
    • Cross-checking with Financial Policies: Validation against internal financial policies or specific rules regarding what types of entries can use which GL codes.
  3. Description Validation:
    • Attachment Match: The AI can compare the GL description with the content of the attached document to ensure they are consistent. For example, if the entry refers to "Consulting Fees" but the attachment shows "Travel Expenses", the system can flag this discrepancy.
    • Standard Description Check: Ensuring that entries match the standard descriptions specified in internal accounting guidelines or corporate documentation.
  4. Attachment Validation:
    • Document Existence Check: Ensuring that required documents (e.g., invoices, contracts, receipts) are attached. If they are missing, an alert is generated.
    • Attachment Type Matching: The system can check whether the correct type of attachment is associated with the entry. For example, if the entry refers to a "Vendor Payment," the attached document can be an invoice or a vendor agreement.
    • Document Consistency: Comparing the details in the attachment (e.g., amounts, dates) with the amounts or other details in the journal entry to verify consistency.
  5. Approval Workflow Validation:
    • Approval Matching: Ensuring that all journal entries have been approved according to the organization’s internal controls. The AI can validate whether the appropriate manager or department head has provided the necessary approval for the entry type.
    • Approval Policy Check: Cross-checking entries against approval thresholds defined by the organization (e.g., entries above $10,000 must have senior management approval).
  6. Data Completeness:
    • Missing Information: Identifying any missing or incomplete fields in the journal entry. For example, ensuring that the "reason for entry" field or "cost center" field is populated.
    • Consistency with Historical Data: Cross-checking the current entry against similar entries in historical data to ensure that the same fields are populated appropriately.
  7. Audit & Compliance Validation:
    • Regulatory Compliance: Validating that the journal entry complies with relevant accounting regulations (e.g., GAAP, IFRS). The AI system can cross-check entries for common non-compliance patterns.
    • Audit Trail: Ensuring that there is a clear and complete audit trail for all parked journal entries, including details on who made the entry, when it was parked, and who approved it.
 

 

 

Solution Approach

  1. AI and Machine Learning Models:
    • Use machine learning algorithms that can be trained on historical data, previous journal entries, and standard accounting practices to recognize patterns of typical errors and anomalies.
    • The system can apply supervised learning techniques, using labeled data (e.g., previous correct and incorrect journal entries), to improve accuracy over time.
  2. Natural Language Processing (NLP):
    • To validate descriptions, attachment content, and other free-form text fields, NLP techniques can be applied to identify mismatches between the journal entry and the attached documentation.
  3. Integration with S/4  Systems:
    • The solution can seamlessly integrate with existing S/4  or accounting systems to "park" the journal entries and raise alerts automatically when discrepancies are found.
  4. Automated Alerts and Notifications:
    • Once anomalies are detected, the system can send automated alerts to relevant personnel (accountants, managers, auditors) with clear descriptions of the issue and suggested corrections.

Key Benefits

  • Improved Accuracy: By automating the validation of manual entries before posting, the AI system can significantly reduce the likelihood of errors.
  • Cost and Time Savings: Automating the review and validation process saves time and money that would otherwise be spent on manual corrections and audits.
  • Compliance and Risk Mitigation: Ensures that all entries comply with financial regulations and internal policies, reducing the risk of non-compliance and potential penalties.
  • Audit Readiness: The system maintains a comprehensive log of all validation checks, providing a clear audit trail for future reviews.

Type of Journal Entry

Description

S/4HANA AI Automation

Standard Entries

Monthly accruals for expenses like utilities or salaries.

S/4HANA uses AI to predict and automate recurring standard entries based on historical data, improving efficiency and accuracy.

Recurring Entries

Lease payments or subscription fees that occur regularly.

AI algorithms in S/4HANA identify patterns and automate the setup and posting of recurring entries, reducing manual intervention.

Adjusting Entries

Year-end adjustments for prepaid expenses or inventory changes.

S/4HANA's AI capabilities assist in suggesting necessary adjustments and automate postings based on predefined rules and criteria.

Accrual Entries

Accruing unpaid invoices for services received but not yet billed.

AI in S/4HANA predicts and accrues expenses and revenues, ensuring timely recognition and minimal manual oversight.

Reclassification Entries

Reclassifying expenses from one department to another.

AI tools help automate reclassifications by analyzing transaction data and suggesting necessary reallocations.

Tax-related Entries

Journal entries related to tax provisions or deferred tax liabilities.

S/4HANA utilizes AI to ensure tax compliance by automating complex calculations and adjustment entries according to tax laws.

Adjustments for Errors

Correcting a previously recorded entry due to misclassification or numerical error.

AI-driven error detection and correction features in S/4HANA help identify discrepancies and propose corrections efficiently.

 

In conclusion, implementing an AI-powered validation system for parked manual journal entries will streamline the accounting process, reduce errors, ensure compliance, and save significant time and effort during month-end, quarter-end, and year-end closing periods.

Srinath Ganesan

entry.title: Connecting Cloud ALM with BPA and configuring, triggering of automation in BPA upon job failure
entry.content:
Logon to BTP cockpit

Navigate to Overview section

Take note of Tenant ID and Subaccount ID

Connecting Cloud ALM to BPA :

Log on to SAP Cloud ALM tenant

Navigate to Administration -> Landscape Management

Click on the Service

Choose Add -> New Service

Enter relevant details as below in the window that pops up

The Root URL and Logon URL are the URL's from the lobby page of BPA. Please omit /lobby before using it in the Root URL and Logon URL.

Click Save.

Scroll to ENDPOINTS

Click Add

Next step is to obtain the Service key

Logon to SAP BTP Cockpit.  

  •  Navigate to the subaccount where SAP Build Process Automation is subscribed.
  • Select Instances and Subscriptions. 
  •  Select the service instance for SAP Build Process Automation.
  •  Select tab Service Keys. 
  •  Select Actions --> Download.

Create API Key

  •  Logon to SAP Build Process Automation. 
  •  Select Control Tower.
  •  Create an API key with scopes trigger_read and trigger_execute for the environments you want to use.
  •  Copy the generated API key.

While generating the API key activate

trigger_read

trigger_execute

Activate for all environments

Save the generated Key on a notepad

Return back to CALM screen and add endpoint

Once setup, Ping connection to test.

Congratulations. You have not successfully connected Cloud ALM with BPA.

As a next step, if a job fails in CALM then it needs to trigger the automation built in BPA. Let's configure this now!

Let's create a trigger in BPA.

Log into BPA

Select project that needs to be triggered upon job failure in CALM

Create -> Process

Click on Add a trigger -> API Trigger

Create API trigger

Add a process step

Choose Automation that needs to be triggered 

Choose the automation that needs to be triggered and Save it

Most important step is to release and deploy it. Else the trigger will not be identified by CALM

Click on Editable and deploy it.

Now let's set up CALM to trigger the automation in BPA upon job failure:

Login to CALM

Navigate to Operations -> Operation Automation

Register Operational Flow -> SAP Build Process Automation

End point gets automatically identified

Process Automation section, choose the Trigger that we built in BPA

Use Case : Job & Automation Monitoring

We now need to set up the Job and Automation Monitoring.

Operations -> Job & Automation Monitoring

Choose System

Choose the job that needs to be monitored

Click on Settings -> System

Click Events -> Add

Start Operational Flow -> Add -> Add Operational Flow

Select the Configured Trigger and Save it

Congratulation, We have now set up connectivity between SAP Cloud ALM and BPA and also configured triggering automation flows in BPA upon job failure.

entry.title: Triggering SAP Build Process Automation Processes from SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Apps
entry.content:
In this blog post, we'll explore the different methods to trigger SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) processes from a SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) application. We will cover triggering via destinations and Event Mesh.

Table of Contents

1. Triggering via Destinations

    • Prerequisites
    • Without Principal Propagation
    • With Principal Propagation

2. Triggering via Event Mesh

    • Setting Up Event Mesh
    • Triggering via Event Mesh
    • Using CloudEvents
    • Testing via Postman

 

 

Triggering via Destinations

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure you have:

  • A CAP application in place with the destination service configured
  • A deployed BPA process
  • A service instance and service key created for SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)

Without Principal Propagation

Follow these steps to trigger a BPA process without principal propagation:

1. Create a destination to the BPA instance using OAuth2ClientCredentials:

    • In your subaccount, navigate to Destinations and create a new destination.
    • Enter the required details such as Name, Type (HTTP), base URL, and select OAuth2ClientCredentials as the Authentication Method.
    • Provide the necessary Client ID, Client Secret, and Token Service URL.

2. Set up the destination in package.json:

    • Define the destination in your CAP application's package.json under the cds.requires section.

 

"cds": {
	"requires": {
		"bpa_destination": {
			"kind": "rest",
			"credentials": {
				"destination": "<destinationname>"
			}
		}
	}
}

 

3. Connect to the destination via cds.connect.to(destinationname):

    • Use the following code snippet in your CAP service or handler file to connect to the destination:

 

const { cds } = require('@sap/cds');
const bpa_destination = await cds.connect.to("<destinationname>");

 

4. Call the API: Use the connection to make a POST request to the BPA API endpoint:

 

 

 

 

let payload = {
	definitionId: "<myProcessID>",
	context: {
		<StringName1>: "<InputString1>",
		<IntegerName2>: <SampleInteger>
      }
};
let oResult = await bpaService.tx(req).post('/v1/workflow-instances', payload);

if (response.status !== 201) {
	throw new Error('Failed to trigger the process.');
}

 

Note: Adding the URL suffix is optional if the full URL is provided in the destination or in the destination configuration inside the package.json

 

Using Principal Propagation

If you need to propagate user information:

1a. Set up trust between subaccounts (if BPA and CAP are in different subaccounts):

  • See SAP Help Documentation for establishing trust between subaccounts.

1b. Create a destination with OAuth2UserTokenExchange:

Similar to the OAuth2ClientCredentials setup, but select OAuth2UserTokenExchange as the Authentication Method.

  1. Verify API call with principal propagation:
    • Ensure the API call is made with the propagated user context from the request body.
  2. Coding remains the same as without principal propagation:
    • Follow the same code snippet as above.

Note: When making changes to the destination, it can take a few minutes until the changes are active. To shorten this timeframe, you can delete and re-create the destination.

 

 

Triggering via Event Mesh

Before trying to trigger events, ensure that the Event Mesh service is correctly set up, including creating a client, service descriptor and at least one queue which subscribes to a topic.

Triggering via Event Mesh

1. Create custom event specification for SAP Build Process Automation

To create a custom event you need to create an event specification that complies with the CloudEvents specification. This file needs to be stored as a .json file. Here is a generic sample of how this event specification could look like: 

 

{
  "asyncapi": "2.0.0",
  "info": {
    "title": "Generic Event",
    "version": "1.0",
    "description": "This event is triggered by a generic rule engine using custom rules."
  },
  "x-api-type": "EVENT",
  "x-shortText": "Informs a subscriber about a relevant event for a process.",
  "x-stateInfo": { "state": "active" },
  "channels": {
    "generic/event/channel": {
      "subscribe": {
        "message": {
          "description": "myMessageDescription",
          "$ref": "#/components/messages/generic_event_v1"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "components": {
    "messages": {
      "generic_event_v1": {
        "name": "myMessageName",
        "summary": "mySummary",
        "description": "myDescription",
        "headers": {
          "properties": {
            "type": {
              "const": "<messageType>"
            },
            "datacontenttype": { "const": "application/json" }
          }
        },
        "payload": {
          "$ref": "#/components/schemas/generic_event_v1"
        },
        "traits": [{ "$ref": "#/components/messageTraits/CloudEventContext" }]
      }
    },
    "schemas": {
      "generic_event_v1": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "PROPERTY_1": { "type": "string" },
          "PROPERTY_2": { "type": "string" },
          "PROPERTY_3": { "type": "string" }
        }
      }
    },
    "messageTraits": {
      "CloudEventContext": {
        "headers": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "specversion": {
              "type": "string",
              "enum": ["1.0"]
            },
            "type": {
              "description": "Type of occurrence which has happened. Often this property is used for routing, observability, policy enforcement, etc.",
              "type": "string",
              "minLength": 1
            },
            "source": { "type": "string" },
            "subject": { "type": "string" },
            "id": { "type": "string" },
            "time": {
              "type": "string",
              "format": "date-time"
            },
            "datacontenttype": {
              "description": "Describe the data encoding format",
              "type": "string",
              "const": "application/json"
            }
          },
          "required": ["id", "specversion", "source", "type"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

 

2. Create custom event trigger inside SAP Build Process Automation

To create a custom event in BPA, you have to perform the following steps:

1. Open the SAP Build lobby.

2. Open Connectors > Actions in the menu.

3. Click the Create button and upload your previously created .json file.

4. Release and publish your event

5. Set your new event as a trigger for your process inside the process editor

 

Setup Event Mesh webhook:

To set up the connection between Event Mesh and SAP Build Process Automation, you need to set up a webhook. Webhooks are POST calls that are automatically triggered for each event inside the queue. See this blog post for more information about webhooks.

The following steps are necessary to set up your webhook:

1. Open the client inside your Event Mesh instance.

2. Click on the webhooks tab.

3. Create a new webhook by providing a name, inserting the webhook URL (your BPA endpoint URL in your service key + "/internal/be/v1/events") and the authentication type OAuth2ClientCredentials using the client key and the client secret from the BPA service key.

4. Exempt the handshake and resume the webhook.

 

Trigger events in CAP using messaging.emit():

Use the messaging.emit() function to emit events from CAP to Event Mesh.

 

const messaging = await cds.connect.to('messaging');

await messaging.emit({
        event: '<eventName>',
        data: <inputDataObject>,
        headers: { type: '<messageType>' },
});

 

Note: The data object needs to comply with the CloudEvents standard. See this blog post for more information.

 

Testing Event Trigger via REST-Client

To test the Event Mesh and Event Trigger setup we can test via REST-Clients (such as Postman). This needs to be configured accordingly:

1. Use the following endpoint for Event Mesh

    • Endpoint: <yourEventMeshEndpoint>

You can find the endpoint in your Event Mesh instance service key under messaging. Make sure it contains the pubsub part and that it looks similar to this one: https://enterprise-messaging-pubsub.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/messagingrest/v1/messages

2. Set the following headers:

    • Content-Type: application/cloudevents+json
    • x-qos: 1
    • x-address: queue:<queueName>

3. Fetch a Bearer Token for authentication:

    • Ensure you have a valid Bearer Token for authorization. Get one using the grant type Authorization Code.

4. Send a POST request with the CloudEvent payload:

    • Construct and send a POST request to the Event Mesh endpoint.

 

{
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "type": "<messageType>",
  "source": "<queue>/manual",
  "id": "740f8e3e-6b99-40ae-bdc3-375277cc2edb",
  "time": "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    // Your SAP Build Process Automation context here
  },
  "datacontenttype": "application/json"
}

 

 

By following these guidelines, you can trigger SAP Build Process Automation processes from a SAP CAP application using various methods. This approach can greatly enhance the integration and automation capabilities within a SAP landscape.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, December 12th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/12/12/sap-developer-news-december-12th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Report from UKISUG

SAP BTP Innobytes November 2024

  • Video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cWVpcjIvZ4Y

CAP November 2024 Release

  • Capire November 2024 Release documentation https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/nov24

Migration resources SAP Business Process Automation

  • The Path Forward: SAP Build Process Automation https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/the-path-forward-sap-build-process-automation/ba-p/13938403

Simplifying SAP BTP Authentication with SAP Passport and Playwrite

  • Blog Post by Marian Zeis (6) Simplifying SAP BTP Authentication in Pipelines with SAP Passport and Playwright | LinkedIn

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 UKISUG
1:14 SAP BTP Innobytes November 2024
1:58 CAP November 2024 Release
4:58 Migration resources SAP Business Process Automation
5:45 Simplifying BTP Authentication

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for December 12th, 2024.

[Antonio, DJ, & Daniel] Hola, SAP developers. We're here at the UK-ISUG Connect in Birmingham, where we've been here with our developer advocates in the SAP TechEd stand, running the developer garage and with our colleagues in the Ask the Experts booth. We've met a ton of people who've come to do tutorials. We've given some sessions, so I've given a session about SAP Build code and SAP Build with the DJ and through the floor we've met a lot of people who follow what we're doing and it has been very exciting. And we've had some great conversations in fact this is the ideal combination of hands-on keyboards and talking to each other face to face and learning from each other and reconnecting with each other. I've got to rush off now because I'm going to deliver another session, a hands-on session, on Terraform provider for SAP BTP. So, it's goodbye from me. And from me. And me. See you later. Bye-bye.

[DJ] The November 2024 edition of SAP BTP InnoBytes is out now with more fresh snackable insights on BTP. This time there's information on planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. There's information on a new starter edition of SAP Integration Suite and on the document information extraction area. There's a new premium edition. Information on that is in this month's InnoBytes. Check the video out. It's only four minutes long. Link, as always, in the description.

[Ajay] Hello everyone. November release of SAP CAP 2024 is now available. So many things have happened since the first release of Getting Started and Cookbook guides. Many of them needed major updates and overhaul. Hence, Caphire now has revised Getting Started guides and also an overview of key concepts. Welcome page, getting started index pages, introduction, what is CAP, best practices and anti-patterns are some of the revised content. Learn more page combines information which was earlier spread across several pages. There is a new guide for aspect oriented modeling and improved CDL definition language reference. Coming to the CDS language and compiler topics, we have updates about the new CDL parser with significant size reduction and planned rollout in three phases. Now there is support for open intervals with add date "Assert.range" by wrapping the min and the max values in parenthesis. NodeJS's updates include a new plugin for RFC. CDS-RFC now enables importing RFC-enabled function modules from SAP S/4 HANA system. New CDS-I18n API can be used consistently for both serving localized SAP Fiori UIs as well as for localized messages at runtime. The default fuzziness threshold used by CAP Node.js is 0.7 and is now made configurable. Besides this, Adder Age "Search.fuzziness" threshold and Adder Age "Search.ranking" annotation is now supported by the CAP Node.js runtime. The new CLI command CDS debug lets you easily debug local or remote Node.js applications in Chrome DevTools. You can now also have automatic handler stubs in Node.js projects for both JavaScript and TypeScript with the command cds add handler. Basically in CAP Node.js, you can now create ECMAScript module projects with cds add esm command. For CAP Java, simpler recursive hierarchies can be realized with aggregate aggregation dot recursive hierarchies annotation. You can now filter and sort by sub-elements of cds.map elements in CAP Java. Enhanced instance-based authorization is supported in CAP Java with introduction of a new configuration option, reject-selected-unauthorized-entity. Last update is about MTX. In June release of Caphire,it was announced that only few annotations are allowed as extensions. You can now add exceptions for these restrictions, but at your own risk, using the annotations property. Please visit the Capphire documentation for further details. You can find the link to the documentation in the description.

[DJ] You probably know that SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA have been retired to be replaced by SAP Business Process Automation. Now you're going to have to do some migration and recently there have been some migration webinars to help you along the way. In Lujain's blog post there are also some resources listed that can support you in this migration and coming soon to this blog post will be the addition of links to these migration webinar replays in case you missed them. So check out Lujain's blog post and happy migrating!

[Thomas] We'd like to point your attention to a recent blog post by Marian Zeis that covers the topic of SAP BTP authentication but in the context of pipelines. He talks about how in automated pipelines can also be often be difficult to handle authentication mechanisms securely and simply and he offers an approach using the SAP Passport instead of certificate based authentication and using the Playwright module to simplify the processing of the passports. He does a really good job of describing the overall details of this solution, some code samples of how to set up the context with the certificate, and how to use the Playwright client certificate login package. He even has links to the GitHub repos and the package authentication, the package details, as as well as a code sample from his own GitHub repository on how to set up and use the scenario that he's describing. So this is a challenge that you face. We think you should definitely check out his blog post and see how he has solved the solution for himself and shared the solution with the community.

entry.title: Santa builds process to distribute Christmas presents
entry.content:
No one would deny that Santa has a complex process to give out presents for Christmas. 

But several key new SAP Build Process Automation features has enabled Santa to automate the process using a true Fusion Team of reindeer, elves, and his wife. Among the features:

  • Scheduled triggers
  • Script task
  • Custom variables
  • Subprocesses
  • Go to task

And the process can actually teach us some of the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation.

Scheduled Triggers

Probably the most obvious feature to help Santa is the scheduled trigger for regular processes. We could rely on one of the elves to send an API call, but the scheduled trigger is much more reliable.

Action and Data Type to Get Children 

The process uses an action to retrieves a list of children.

We first created a data type to represent a child (name string and a Boolean on whether they were nice or naughty).

Then we created custom variables, one to hold the full list of children, and another to hold the current child in our iteration.

Then we run an action to get the children.

Script Task and GoTo for Iterating Children

Now we create a script task to move the last child into the current child variable, and to remove that child from the full list.

And finally we create an iteration sequence by a Go To back to the start, where we have a condition to see if the list has more children, and 

Subprocesses

We create subprocesses like "Prepare Reindeer and Sled" since we are likely to need to do that during the year, and we create subprocesses like "Deliver via Chimney" in order to simplify and isolate our logic.

Obvious, in a more realistic case we would have created additional logic to review all the actions of a child to determine if they were nice or naughty, as Santa "sees you when you're sleepin'" and "knows when you're awake", and likely is aware of all actions of a children, so we might use a Decision to make that determination.

And we might create an automation to deal with the complexity of building the toys, handling emails in Outlook from kids wanting presents, and looking for sales on toys on the web. 

🎅🎄❄🤶⛪🕎🕌🤗🦌

Hoping the holiday season and the new year brings you peace and love and happiness, and everything you dream about.

entry.title: Wrapping up 2024 for Application Development, Automation and Integration on SAP BTP: What a year!
entry.content:
2024 has been an incredibly busy year for our application development, automation and integration portfolio on the SAP Business Technology Platform. As the year draws to a close, I wanted to take some time to reflect on our achievements. Indeed, I am very grateful to the people in my teams who have worked very hard to deliver so many improvements to our solutions, and I would like to say a big thank you to all of them.

Of course, you've heard a lot about Joule, our AI co-pilot, over the course of the year. In fact, we have been integrating AI in general, and Generative AI with Joule in particular, into all components of our portfolio, as we announced earlier. And we demonstrated our AI capabilities at SAP TechEd two months ago. This deployment of Business AI has generated great interest from our customers and partners, as well as analysts.

But we've also introduced many new features and enhancements, which I'm proud to present in detail below.

SAP Build

In terms of application development and automation, in addition to embedding AI in all of our solutions, our biggest improvement in 2024 was to enrich SAP Build with pro-code capabilities. Indeed, we announced SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd 2023, made available in March this year, and we recently added ABAP Environments to SAP Build. SAP Build now allows business users and developers to work across their low-code, Java/JavaScript and ABAP projects from a unique starting point, the SAP Build lobby, benefitting of seamless interoperability capabilities fostering fusion development.

With this, SAP Build is now THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development. And SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions with SAP Build directly from the ERP business application, thanks to our new Extensibility Wizard for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This year, we have worked hard to improve the integration of the SAP Build components, both with other components of SAP BTP and with other SAP solutions: for example, SAP Build Process Automation is now integrated with SAP Build Apps and also with Advanced Event Mesh, applications created with SAP Build Apps can be deployed in SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Work Zone supports multi-tenancy.

Finally, I would like to mention that we have improved the usability of SAP Build, for example with a new process layout and script task editor in SAP Build Process Automation and guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone.

This image summarizes all the improvements we delivered this year:

A few weeks ago, Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP). I would like to thank the SAP Build team for achieving this great result!

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – November 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – July 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q2 2024 Edition
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Seamless Integration of Processes and Apps
  • What’s New with SAP Build Process Automation: January 2024

SAP Integration Suite

In 2024 we have also improved SAP Integration Suite in many different areas:

Regarding SAP PI/PO migration, which is a topic on which I published a blog post earlier this year to explain Advanced Event Mesh and Edge Integration Cell, we introduced a Pipeline concept and new rules added for migration assessment.

Edge Integration Cell is now available on Google Cloud and Red Hat OpenShift. It supports outgoing connections through a HTTP proxy and provides offline availability during periods of temporary connectivity loss.

This year, Event Mesh became a capability of SAP Integration Suite, which also allows direct consumption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Events and distributed tracing, from sending to receiving applications across one or multiple event brokers.

We have been investing heavily in improving developer efficiency and operational excellence. A few of Gen AI Increments that we recently launched were to accelerate development of integration flows with Gen AI and analyze APIs using API Anomaly detection in API Management

You can also accelerate connectivity with SAP Integration Suite using new adapters for Coupa, NetSuiteERP, SnowFlake, and Amazon DynamoDB, SMB, Jira, Amazon EventBridge, HubSpot, Anaplan with many more planned to be released soon.

We enhanced Cloud Integration and API Management user experience with SAP Horizon theme. Enriched the Cloud Integration capability with the ability to inspect content size for all the integration artifacts, manage access at the package level, validate integration flow design as per the guidelines in integration flow editor.

Furthermore, API Management now supports multiple target endpoints in API proxy tooling and recently launched Developer Hub for search, discovery and consumption of APIs. We also launched oData provisioning as a new capability.

We have onboarded 25+ partners with a contract and further enriched our marketplace with 53+ new partner content in particular 7 new APIs, 4 Processes, 20+ Integration packages and 22+ Validated partner use cases added on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Finally, B2B Scenarios for Trading Partner Management (TPM) and Integration Advisor have also been enriched with functionalities like archiving of Payload data for Trading Partner Management scenarios, EDI flat file payloads can be directly used to simulate mappings, for easier access to envelope fields these can be added in the Integration Advisor, simplified mass-maintenance in TPM, support for custom SOA messages in Integration Advisor, new Tradacoms message standard.

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Whats New For SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – June & July 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – Spring 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – February 2024
  • What's New for SAP Integration Suite - January 2024

Feel free to follow our #WhatsNewInSAPIntegrationSuite on SAP Community and register for our webinar for updates on upcoming innovations!

Finally, I’m also proud to share that with SAP Integration Suite SAP has been named a Leader for 4th consecutive time by Gartner® in its 2024 Magic Quadrant™ for iPaaS. In addition, SAP is a Leader in IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Business-to-Business Middleware 2024 Vendor Assessment.

Kudos to the team for these achievements!

What’s for 2025?

As you’ve seen above, we delivered a lot of new features and enhancements in 2024. And for sure we will continue in 2025!

You can find out what we have planned for next year on our public roadmap for application development, automation and integration on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

With that, I wish you a Merry Christmas and very happy holidays!  🎅🏻🎄

entry.title: Posting attachments to ECC from SAP BTP Cloud Foundry workflow [Build process automation]
entry.content:
Hi All,

We had a requirement to post attachments with different extensions to backend [ECC]. Required functionality was to fetch file content for respective posted invoice from azure, which can be one or multiple files with different extensions, convert it in acceptable ECC format and post it to ECC.

When I was implementing this functionality, I couldn’t find any straightforward blog which would help me explain how to send attachments from workflow to ECC or any other backend system. Hence, I explored some other options, made it successful and thought I should share so that it can be useful and can be referred by others who want to achieve similar kind of functionality in their projects. Hope this helps.

We had a requirement where we wanted to send attachments to ECC. This requirement was to post attachments to backend only if invoice posting is successful.

Here our complete logic flow is as below:

  • User will raise invoice request where user has flexibility to add documents with any extension.
  • Request will be stored in azure and BTP workflow will be triggered for the same for posting the invoice to backend.
  • Once invoice posting is successful, workflow will fetch the attachment file content, and as is send to ECC with required parameters against related invoice.

 

Steps to add logic flow for posting attachments to ECC.

  • First, I am checking if posting of invoice is successful, as we want to send attachments only for success scenario and not for failures

 

 

 

  • If it’s successful, fetch the files from invoice request, one invoice can have multiple files with different extensions.

 

 

 

 

  • Fetch the file content from azure by sending file name in required payload format to API call. Collect the response from azure in response variable for next step.

 

 

 

 

 

  • Prepare payload for posting document to ECC. Header parameters and payload content is what we ll require to send to entity set.

 

 

 

 

 

Header parameters as above.

And file content we will send as is from azure response variable to “Request variable” section to avoid any corruption or additional quotes which gets added due to reassignment.

 

  • Once posting step is completed, we will handle the notification for success/error. And in next step we will check if files are remaining to post.

 

  • Then, we’ll check flag [which we can set by shifting or removing files form original file array of one or multiple files] for remaining documents, as we are posting one document at a time. Once all files are posted, we will complete the workflow.

 

 

 

  • From backend we must convert the file content [which is base64] in X-string to process it further. 

Once all files are posted in backend, we can view all posted attachments against invoice as below.

 

 

Using this approach, you can post files easily to backend, as well you can view attached files from backend.

 

 

That is all folks, this is how I achieved the posting files to backend. You can modify the flow and steps as per your own requirement. Good Luck!

entry.title: How to Use Formulas in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
In SAP Build Apps, transformation formulas play a vital role in dynamically manipulating data within applications. Like Excel formulas, these predefined functions and expressions allow you to build logic seamlessly without the need for custom coding. By leveraging transformation formulas, you can efficiently transform data for UI bindings, API responses, and other app logic requirements.

Formulas serve as one of the primary component binding types in SAP Build Apps. This means they can be applied in almost any part of the application to bind and manipulate data dynamically. For instance, when binding a "Text" or "Dropdown" component value, you can simply select the binding icon in the Properties tab and choose "Formula" as the binding type. This flexibility extends to tasks such as validating data fetched from an API, validating the data, Changing the Formats of display data, String and Arithmetic functions, etc., Using formulas, you can access all existing variables in the application that can then be modified.

We developed a prototype for creating a Business Partner using SAP Build Apps integrated with SAP Build Process Automation. Here, SAP On-Prem systems are connected to read data from the Business Partner API and display it as Value Helps for dropdown values

 

 

Here’s an overview of common transformation formulas that we used in SAP Build Apps for reading the data from backend and displaying as value helps:

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Build Apps provides an extensive range of built-in functions to simplify transformations, covering categories like:

  • Text Operations: To Check if a value exists, Select Query , or match patterns using regex.
  • Date Functions: Manipulate and format dates dynamically to the local format.
  • Color and Object Handling: Dynamically update colors based on conditions, objects, and other data types.
  • Arithmetic Operations: Perform calculations and computations directly such as sum, absolute values, is_even, is_odd, divide, multiply etc,.

 

In addition to built-in formulas, you can design custom transformation formulas to handle specific functionalities within your app. Here are a few common use cases:To map an input field of Dropdown type, we can map the data that needs to read from a datasource

  • Mapping Data to a Dropdown Input Field -  To bind a dropdown component to a field in data source as a value help,we can map the desired fields as:

MAP(data.ZCDV_BusinessPartnerRole1, {label:item.BPRoleText,value:item.BusinessPartnerRole})

 

 

  • Displaying Unique Fields Without Duplicates - To ensure a dropdown contains only unique values and no duplicates with different combinations

UNIQUE_BY_KEY(MAP(data.ZCDV_BupaCountry1,{label:item.CountryText, value:item.Country}), "value")

  • Fetching State/Region Data Based on Selected Country input - To dynamically filter and display regions or states in dropdown based on the selected country

 

MAP(SELECT(data.ZCDV_BupaCountry1, item.Country == pageVars.bp_inputs.country), {label: item.RegionText, value: item.RegionName})

 

  • Checking if Input is Empty - To validate whether an input field is blank or contains a value for conditional processing

IS_EMPTY(pageVars.bp_inputs.country)

 

  • Text transformations– Converting Variables to Uppercase/Lowercase using text transformations.Also, we can validate pattern for email using IS_EMAIL or REGEX functions.

UPPERCASE(data.A_BusinessPartner1[0].Initials)

Note: In SAP Build Apps, custom logic or formulas cannot be directly written for all data sources. Only APIs that are exposed can be utilized for any kind of data transformation or manipulation.

I hope this blog post provides you with a understanding of formulas in SAP Build Apps and how they can be leveraged to enhance data manipulation within your applications.

entry.title: MDG Cloud Ready Mode: Custom Workflow template in SAP build process automation with S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi All,

Season's Greetings!!

As we enter 2025, technology space is evolving at a rapid pace with GenAI, Agentic LLM's findings it's way in each and every enterprise level application. With Joule taking a bigger role in all SAP cloud applications, MDG has also been integrated with Joule for cloud ready mode with S4HANA 2023 FPS02. Markus discusses this in his blog Key Presentations about SAP Master Data Governance... - SAP Community.

In the blog MDG Cloud ready Mode: Integration of S/4HANA with SAP Build process automation & SAP Build work zone I talked about step-by-step details on how to set up the integration between S/4HANA on-prem to SAP BTP build process automation. In this blog we are going to explore how to set up our own workflow template in build process automation and link it with MDG process template.

INTRODUCTION:

In any MDG implementation, a big part of the design phase is to understand the workflow requirements and create custom workflows in BRF+ to cater to these requirements. In the MDG cloud ready mode also SAP has provided option to create your own Workflow templates. This has to be done in SAP build process automation.

There are however some limitations in terms of the current capabilities. A simple example is currently the control once transferred to MDC process model, can't be sent back to build process automation workflow. 

The way in which i have setup the custom workflow is little different from what is documented in the SAP help page. You can find it here.Custom Workflow and Visibility (Optional) | SAP Help Portal.

Essentially, I have not taken the standard Workflow template application in Build process automation as base, instead I have created completely different workflow.

PS: I followed the tutorial Create an API Trigger for the Process | SAP Tutorials to create the Build process automation project, so you can refer to this tutorial for step-by-step details.

MAIN CONTENT:

1. Create a blank WF project in build process automation. This workflow will be a trigger-based workflow, where trigger will be provided by the MDG process for cloud ready mode. 

2. For a trigger-based API, we need to define the input data structure of the API which is a data type. MasterDataProcess is the data type which was created with multiple fields.

3.  We have to create the process input to the API trigger; in this case I have kept only the processID as the mandatory input for the trigger.

4. Now we need to create the process artifact, which will take the Approve or Reject decision. In this case again it's only approval, rejection scenario can also be built on the build process automation side. Approve1 is the decision form 

 

 

The add quoates step, is technical step as the payload to S4HANA need to contain quotes for the WF ID instance. Currently its going without quotes, this might be overcome with future releases.

4. Once this is completed, we need to invoke a particular service SWF_CPWF_NOTIFICATION_SRV which will call S/4HANA and send the Workflow decision and ensure the process continues further. 

This service needs to be added as a dependency to your build process automation project.

This service expects SAP client and the workflow instanceID as input.

5. Once all these steps are completed the build process automation project along with the API triggers need to be deployed.

6. From the monitoring tab we can locate our custom workflow which is deployed and copy the ID.

7. Create a custom process template and in step workflow column paste the ID from step6.

Once this step is completed, the integration between the custom workflow template and the S4HANA MDG cloud ready mode process template is completed.

9. Below are the 2 methods which are useful for troubleshooting any integration issues with your custom workflow.


CONCLUSION:

This blog outlines few of the important steps, needed to deploy a custom workflow template in Build process automation and integrate it with MDG cloud ready mode process template on S4HANA on-prem.

MDG cloud ready mode is the next gen deployment option for Master data governance following the cloud first approach of SAP.

Hope you this blog provides some guidance during your MDG cloud ready mode implementation.

Please share your thoughts and questions if any.

Regards

Anirban

SAP Master Data Governance   SAP Build Process Automation   SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition   SAP S/4HANA   SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition SAP Business Technology Platform   ABAP Cloud   SAP S/4HANA business partner

entry.title: SAP Build产品功能更新目录
entry.content:
➡️SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 4 季度版本

亮点功能：

  • SAP Build流程自动化在流程中通过 Actions 使用 RFC / BAPI

  • SAP Build Code 移动开发工具包（MDK）中的生成式 AI 功能增强
  • Joule 与 Work Zone 标准版的集成
  • 引导式流程向导

 

➡️SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 3 季度版本

亮点功能：

  • 在SAP S/4HANA Cloud中创建扩展流程
  • 跨 SAP Build Work Zone 和 Process Automation 的集成引导式体验

 

➡️SAP Build 的新增功能 – 2024 年第 2 季度版本 

亮点功能：

  • SAP Build Apps 与 SAP Build Process Automation 之间的集成功能
  • SAP Task Center连接第三方任务提供商
  • 从 SAP Intelligent RPA 轻松迁移到 SAP Build Process Automation

 

➡️SAP Build Process Automation 2024 年 1 月新功能详解

亮点功能：

  • 在垂直布局流程编辑器中使用布局的自动化、决策和流程可见性
  • 增强录制器中的不同类型的设置活动选项

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q4 2024 edition of our "What's New "blog series. SAP Build is a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This quarter, we're excited to highlight many new releases, including AI-driven features that transform your ability to automate and innovate. Let's dive in!

Below, you will find product-specific highlights for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation

Create and Manage Process Variants

SAP Build Process Automation now allows you to create and manage process variants. With pre-defined process steps and guardrails, process experts and professional developers can model different variants for different user groups using a configuration-only experience. By defining variant conditions and actions, workflows can be adapted to your specific business needs. With this feature, you can manage tailored workflows for various scenarios without impacting the overall process template. Learn how to create and manage process variants in the SAP Help Portal.

Consume RFC / BAPI in a Process via Actions

To help you extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs. Utilizing the BAPI node library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. Easily search and browse RFC actions to locate the necessary functions for your workflows, maintain system integrity, and reduce complexity.

 

SAP Build Code

Generative AI Capabilities in the Mobile Development Kit

Enhanced generative AI capabilities for the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) can now help you simplify your app creation process. Developers can:

  • Generate Application Logic: Automatically produce metadata-driven logic, reducing manual coding efforts.
  • Create Forms and Workflows: Quickly develop forms and workflows with AI assistance, accelerating development.
  • Build Robust Mobile Solutions: Leverage AI to minimize manual effort, allowing focus on delivering impactful solutions.

Learn more in this blog by @BirgitS. 

SAPUI5 Development with TypeScript and UI5 Linter

AI-Driven Migration to TypeScript with Joule: Joule, the AI assistant integrated into SAP Build Code, now enables a seamless migration of SAPUI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. TypeScript offers enhanced development efficiency, providing a structured and scalable approach to building SAPUI5 apps. Learn more about this powerful capability in this blog by @martinhaeuser.

Release of UI5 Linter v1.0: SAP Build Code introduces UI5 Linter v1.0, a tool designed to ensure code quality and alignment with best practices. This release empowers developers to write cleaner, more maintainable SAPUI5 code, enhancing the overall development lifecycle.

A New Experience for Extending SAP Fiori Apps

Adaptation Editor

Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 Projects: SAP Build Code now features the Adaptation Editor, a new tool for extending SAPUI5 applications. Replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, the Adaptation Editor is available in SAP Business Application Studio, offering an intuitive and consistent experience across environments. Developers can leverage this updated tool for streamlined SAPUI5 extensions with improved performance. For details, see the announcement blog by @OliverGraeff. 

Adaptation vs. Extension Projects: SAPUI5 flexibility supports both adaptation and extension projects, enabling tailored app modifications for developers, key users, and end users. Adaptation projects, now the strategic approach for extending SAPUI5 apps, provide advanced tools for code-based customization. To learn about the differences and benefits of these project types, explore this comparison blog.

SAP Build Apps

Additional HTTP Request Headers for BTP Destinations

Gain greater control and flexibility when integrating external services with SAP Build Apps. You can now configure additional HTTP request headers for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) destinations, enhancing integration security and customization. The new flow function allows you to upload binary files using multipart/form-data as request body type. To learn more, see HTTP Destination Request flow function reference documentation.

A New Top Navigation Bar

SAP Build Apps now has an updated toolbar and navigation menu for a more intuitive experience. Frequently used features like Variables are now accessible directly from the toolbar. Tabs like "UI Canvas" and "Data" have been renamed to "User Interface" and "Integrations." At the same time, less-used options such as Theme and Navigation are grouped under "App Settings" to declutter the interface. Actions like Preview and Publish are prominently placed in the upper-right corner, simplifying release versioning and deployment. For more details, read the feature review blog post by @Dan_Wroblewski.

SAP Build Work Zone

Integration with Joule for Work Zone Standard Edition

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities. This integration delivers intelligent workspace recommendations to boost efficiency, proactive insights tailored to tasks and priorities, and streamlined workflows to improve collaboration and focus. For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, check out @Yumnahabbasi's blog post.

Guided Process Wizard

The Guided Process Wizard combines SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation to simplify creating structured, step-by-step workflows. With this integration, you can design guided processes in Process Automation and deploy them as wizards in Work Zone. Build customizable workflows with dynamic UI elements like cards and widgets to streamline complex onboarding and invoice processing tasks. Guided experiences are included in the SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. Read more in the FAQ blogpost by @Yumnahabbasi.

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • What's New Viewer
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Road Map Explorer

To stay updated with the latest announcements and innovations, follow the SAP Builders Community and converse with fellow SAP Builders.

We look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve your goals!

entry.title: SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 4 季度版本
entry.content:
更快、更智能、更高效地构建

欢迎阅读我们2024年第四季度的“产品新功能”博客系列。本文为翻译文，原文链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition，原文作者JesseSukhbayar。

SAP Build 是一款业务应用程序开发和自动化解决方案，提供了一套全面的低代码、专业代码和生成式 AI 工具。本季度，我们很高兴为大家介绍许多新版本，其中包括 AI 驱动的功能，这些功能将彻底改变您的自动化和创新能力。让我们一起来看看吧！

以下内容将为您介绍以下产品的具体亮点：

  • SAP Build Process Automation

  • SAP Build Code

  • SAP Build Apps

  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build 流程自动化

创建和管理流程变体

SAP Build 流程自动化现在支持创建和管理流程变体。通过预定义的流程步骤和防护机制，流程专家和专业开发人员可以使用纯配置的方式为不同的用户群体建模不同的流程变体。通过定义变体条件和操作，工作流可以根据您的具体业务需求进行调整。借助此功能，您可以为各种场景管理定制化的工作流，而不会影响整体流程模板。

您可以在 SAP 帮助门户 中了解如何创建和管理流程变体。

在流程中通过 Actions 使用 RFC / BAPI

为了帮助您扩展 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 私有版和本地部署解决方案，您现在可以基于 RFC / BAPI 创建 Actions。通过利用 BAPI 节点库，您可以直接集成 RFC Actions，从而简化集成流程。您可以轻松搜索和浏览 RFC Actions，以找到工作流所需的必要功能，同时维护系统完整性并降低复杂性。

这一功能使您能够更高效地将 SAP S/4HANA 的功能集成到自动化流程中，从而提升业务操作的灵活性和效率。

SAP Build Code

移动开发工具包（MDK）中的生成式 AI 功能增强

移动开发工具包（MDK）的生成式 AI 功能现已增强，可以帮助您简化应用程序创建过程。开发人员可以：

  1. 生成应用程序逻辑：自动生成基于元数据的逻辑，减少手动编码工作量。

  2. 创建表单和工作流：在 AI 的协助下快速开发表单和工作流，加速开发进程。

  3. 构建强大的移动解决方案：利用 AI 减少手动操作，专注于交付具有影响力的解决方案。

通过这些功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建移动应用，同时将更多精力投入到业务价值的实现中。

了解更多详情，请阅读 @BirgitS 的博客 。

SAPUI5 开发：TypeScript 和 UI5 Linter

使用 Joule 进行 AI 驱动的 TypeScript 迁移

Joule 是集成到 SAP Build Code 中的 AI 助手，现在支持将 SAPUI5 应用程序从 JavaScript 无缝迁移到 TypeScript。TypeScript 提供了更高的开发效率，为构建 SAPUI5 应用程序提供了一种结构化且可扩展的方法。了解更多关于这一强大功能的信息，请阅读 @martinhaeuser 的博客 。

UI5 Linter v1.0 发布

SAP Build Code 推出了 UI5 Linter v1.0，这是一款旨在确保代码质量并与最佳实践保持一致的工具。此版本使开发人员能够编写更清晰、更易维护的 SAPUI5 代码，从而提升整体开发生命周期的效率。

通过这些新功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建高质量的 SAPUI5 应用程序，同时减少错误并提高代码的可维护性。

扩展 SAP Fiori 应用程序的全新体验

适用于 SAPUI5 项目的 Adaptation Editor

SAP Build Code 现在推出了 Adaptation Editor，这是一款用于扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的新工具。Adaptation Editor 取代了 SAPUI5 Visual Editor，并在 SAP Business Application Studio 中提供，为跨环境开发提供了直观且一致的体验。开发人员可以利用这一更新后的工具，以更高的性能简化 SAPUI5 扩展。详情请参阅 @OliverGraeff 的博客 。

适应项目与扩展项目

SAPUI5 的灵活性支持适应项目（Adaptation Projects）和扩展项目（Extension Projects），使开发人员、关键用户和最终用户能够对应用程序进行定制化修改。适应项目现已成为扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的战略性方法，提供了基于代码定制的高级工具。要了解这些项目类型的区别和优势，请参阅 这篇博客 。

通过这些新工具和方法，开发人员可以更灵活、高效地扩展和定制 SAPUI5 应用程序，满足多样化的业务需求。

SAP Build Apps

为 BTP 目标服务添加额外的 HTTP 请求

在将外部服务与 SAP Build Apps 集成时，您现在可以配置额外的 HTTP 请求头，从而获得更高的控制性和灵活性。这一功能增强了集成安全性和定制化能力。此外，新的流函数（flow function）支持使用 multipart/form-data 作为请求体类型上传二进制文件。

要了解更多详细信息，请参阅 HTTP Destination Request 流函数参考文档。

通过这些更新，开发人员可以更安全、更灵活地集成外部服务，并支持更复杂的文件上传场景。

全新的顶部导航栏

SAP Build Apps 现在提供了更新的工具栏和导航菜单，为用户带来更直观的体验。常用的功能（如“变量”）现在可以直接从工具栏访问。同时，部分标签名称进行了更新，例如：

  • “UI Canvas” 更名为 “用户界面”

  • “Data” 更名为 “集成”

此外，使用频率较低的选项（如“主题”和“导航”）被归类到 “应用设置” 中，以减少界面杂乱感。常用操作（如“预览”和“发布”）则被显著地放置在右上角，简化了版本管理和部署流程。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Dan_Wroblewski 的功能评测博客 。

通过这些改进，SAP Build Apps 的用户界面更加简洁高效，帮助开发人员更专注于应用程序的设计和开发。

SAP Build Work Zone

Joule 与 Work Zone 标准版的集成

Joule 现已集成到 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版 中，为工作区提供了 AI 驱动的强大功能。这一集成带来了以下优势：

  • 智能工作区推荐：提升工作效率。

  • 主动洞察：根据任务和优先级提供定制化建议。

  • 简化的工作流：改善协作并帮助用户更专注于关键任务。

通过 Joule 的 AI 能力，用户可以更高效地管理工作区，并获得个性化的支持。

要了解更多关于 Joule 如何改变您的工作区的示例，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的博客 。

引导式流程向导

引导式流程向导（Guided Process Wizard）结合了 SAP Build Work Zone 和 SAP Build 流程自动化，简化了创建结构化、分步工作流的过程。通过这一集成，您可以在流程自动化中设计引导式流程，并将其作为向导部署到 Work Zone 中。

您可以利用动态 UI 元素（如卡片和小部件）构建可定制的工作流，从而简化复杂的入职和发票处理等任务。引导式体验功能包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 高级版 和 SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone 中。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的 FAQ 博客。

 

了解更多

如需查看完整的新功能列表，请访问以下资源：

  • What's New Viewer（新功能查看器）

  • SAP 帮助门户

  • SAP Road Map Explorer（ SAP 路线图探索器）

为了及时了解最新公告和创新动态，请关注 SAP Builders Community，并与社区中的其他 SAP Builders 交流互动。

entry.title: The secret to better "looking" tasks in SAP Build Process Automation: Customizing My Inbox
entry.content:
When working with workflow applications, having access to the right information at the right time is critical. The My Inbox app in SAP Build Process Automation is a key tool for managing and interacting with workflow tasks.

While many are familiar with adding custom parameters in classic workflow scenarios using SAP Business Application Studio, fewer know that similar enhancements are possible in SAP Build Process Automation. 

 

Yes, you can add custom attributes to tasks in the My Inbox app in SAP Build Process Automation. This blog will guide you through the steps to achieve that, covering everything from syntax to UI customization. But before diving into the technical steps, let’s take a moment to understand the business value of this enhancement. 

Business Value of Adding Custom Attributes

Customizing the My Inbox app to display additional attributes isn’t just about improving the appearance—it brings significant business benefits: 

Faster Task Identification: 

By showing key information directly in the task list, users can quickly identify the task they need without having to open each one individually. This reduces time spent searching through tasks and increases productivity. 

Enhanced Search Capabilities: 

When custom attributes are added, they become searchable through the My Inbox app’s search box.
